commit.message:
feat: build PolyPilot against local copilot-sdk from source (#566)

## What

Adds a zero-config way to build PolyPilot against a local copilot-sdk
checkout from source, using a gitignored `Directory.Build.props.local`
override instead of rewriting tracked files.

## How it works

1. `Directory.Build.props` imports `Directory.Build.props.local` if it
exists
2. `Directory.Build.targets` checks for `CopilotSdkProjectPath` — if
set, it removes the `GitHub.Copilot.SDK` package reference and adds a
`ProjectReference` to the local SDK project instead
3. `use-local-sdk.sh` writes/removes that local override file
4. `Directory.Build.props.local` is gitignored — no tracked files are
modified

## Usage

```bash
# Activate local SDK (defaults to ../copilot-sdk)
./use-local-sdk.sh

# Or with a custom path
./use-local-sdk.sh /path/to/copilot-sdk

# Revert to NuGet package
./use-local-sdk.sh --revert
```

This only affects the **compile-time SDK reference**. Runtime CLI
selection (Global vs Built-in) still comes from PolyPilot settings.

## What changed from the original approach

The original version used `sed` to rewrite tracked `nuget.config` and
`.csproj` files, packed a local nupkg, and used `--assume-unchanged` to
hide the changes. That was fragile and platform-specific.

The new version:
- writes a single gitignored file (`Directory.Build.props.local`)
- uses MSBuild's native `ProjectReference` swap via
`Directory.Build.targets`
- does not modify any tracked files
- does not pack a local nupkg — builds the SDK from source alongside
PolyPilot

---------

chore: upgrade lodash to ^4.18.1 and @types/lodash to ^4.17.24 (#5182)

## Summary

Upgrades lodash and its type definitions to the latest versions across
all packages in the monorepo, and switches from `~` (patch-only) to `^`
(minor+patch) version range prefix.

## Changes

| Package | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `lodash` | `~4.17.20` | `^4.18.1` |
| `@types/lodash` | `~4.17.16` | `^4.17.24` |

### Affected packages (7)
- `@autorest/core`
- `@autorest/modelerfour`
- `@autorest/openapi-to-typespec`
- `@autorest/configuration`
- `@azure-tools/datastore`
- `@azure-tools/deduplication`
- `@azure-tools/yaml`

merge: fix pgx interval encoding in retention jobs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use make_interval(days => $1) instead of string concat for interval

pgx v5 infers parameter OID from SQL context. The || operator expects
both operands to be text (OID 25), but we passed Go int values, causing:
  'unable to encode 365 into text format for text (OID 25)'

Replace ($1 || ' days')::INTERVAL with make_interval(days => $1) which
explicitly expects an integer parameter — matching Go's int type.

fix: catch Throwable for GpuDelegate init to handle NoClassDefFoundError

GpuDelegateFactory$Options missing at runtime causes a NoClassDefFoundError
which is an Error not an Exception — the narrow catch was letting it escape
and crashing the app. Widen to Throwable so we fall back to CPU cleanly.

* fix: keyboard shortcuts, MCP config refresh, session links, spec accuracy (#125)

* fix: wire keyboard shortcut preferences into runtime bindings

Settings UI already let users remap keyboard shortcuts (stored as
Ctrl+N format in preferences.keyboardShortcuts), but CommandPalette
used hardcoded display-format shortcuts (⌘N) and App.tsx hardcoded
Ctrl+K for the palette toggle. Runtime bindings never consumed the
user's configured preferences.

Changes:
- useKeyboardShortcuts: unified parser handles both settings (Ctrl+X)
  and display (⌘X) formats with full Alt modifier support; exports
  settingsToDisplayShortcut, matchesShortcutConfig, COMMAND_SHORTCUT_MAP

  command's shortcut dynamically with defensive fallbacks
- App.tsx: palette toggle uses shared matchesShortcutConfig instead of
  duplicated inline parser
- ActivityBar: tooltip shows user-configured palette shortcut
- 19 new tests covering settings format parsing, Alt modifiers,
  display conversion, matchesShortcutConfig, and exact modifier matching

* fix: refresh MCP configs when plugin settings are updated

When plugin settings change via PUT /plugin/{id}/settings, the MCP
config files were not being refreshed — setting variable substitution
in MCP server args/env would remain stale until plugin reload.

Added RefreshMcpConfigsAsync to IPluginHost interface, implemented in
PluginHost (delegates to McpPluginConfigManager.RefreshPluginConfigsAsync),
and called from PluginController.UpdateSettings after settings save.

commit.message:
fix: improve review skill to reduce review cycles (#36)

## Problem

PR #35 took 6 review cycles to converge. Two root causes:

1. **Bot reviewed line-by-line, not system-level.** Each round found 2-4
issues on specific lines but didn't trace implications. Fixes to those
lines created new edge cases caught in the next round.

2. **Fixer (me) did the bare minimum.** Fixed exactly what was pointed
at without stepping back to think about downstream effects — the exact
anti-pattern we then wrote instructions against.

## Fix

Added 'Review Approach' section to the code-review skill:
- **Trace the full flow** — follow data through the system, check every
caller
- **Think about implications** — if recommending 'raise instead of log',
flag what happens to callers
- **Batch related findings** — same class of issue in multiple places →
one comment, not five rounds
- **Front-load everything** — aim for one review round, not five

The implement skill already got 'Responding to Review Feedback'
guidelines in PR #35. This completes the other side of the equation.

---------

commit.message:
Add auto-reconnect investigation: zero-touch node recovery approaches

Research 6 approaches for automatic dandroid1 recovery after
hibernate/reboot without manual intervention:

1. Windows Task Scheduler ✅ — trigger on wake event, launch WSL
2. WSL systemd services ✅ — auto-start when WSL boots (but WSL
   doesn't survive hibernate, needs Task Scheduler to relaunch)
3. Devtunnel persistent auth ⚠️ — no service principal support,
   token lasts 'several days', GitHub auth is more reliable
4. Windows Service wrapper ⚠️ — over-engineered vs Task Scheduler
5. Dev Box customization ⚠️ — only runs during creation, not wake
6. Alternative tunnels 🤔 — Tailscale eliminates auth pain entirely

Recommended: Task Scheduler + systemd + reconnect hooks (Tiers 1-3)
with Tailscale as the nuclear option (Tier 4).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: replace create_all() with Alembic migrations

Replaces the one-way-door Base.metadata.create_all() startup call with
Alembic — the standard SQLAlchemy migration tool. This allows schema
changes (ALTER TABLE, new columns, indexes, constraints) to be applied
safely and repeatably across all environments.

Setup:
- alembic init with render_as_batch=True (required for SQLite ALTER TABLE)
- env.py reads DATABASE_URL from app settings — single source of truth,
  no credential duplication in alembic.ini
- file_template uses timestamp prefix for readable, sortable filenames
- alembic.ini sqlalchemy.url placeholder clearly documented as unused

Initial migration (4cb31083d119):
- Full create_initial_schema covering all 10 tables: users, patients,
  protocols, execution_plans, patient_access, test_sessions, audit_logs,
  used_refresh_tokens, protocol_test_mapping, patient_protocol_assignments
- Generated against empty DB so fresh installs work correctly
- Existing databases stamped at head (alembic stamp head) — no DDL run

main.py:
- _run_migrations() calls alembic upgrade head on every startup
- alembic.ini resolved by absolute path relative to main.py so it works
  regardless of uvicorn working directory
- Base/engine imports removed from main.py (no longer needed for create_all)

Tests: unaffected — conftest.py still uses Base.metadata.create_all() for
in-memory SQLite speed; that is the correct pattern for unit/integration tests.

Future schema changes:
  alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'add_foo_column'
  alembic upgrade head
  # For rollback:
  alembic downgrade -1

r600: add compute sysval blueprint, expand image robustness design

terakan_compute_sysval.h (419 lines): comprehensive compute system value
lowering — TGID/TID via SPI VGPRs, WorkGroupSize via literal fold,
NumWorkGroups via bank 15 driver prefix (bytes 16-27), GlobalID via ALU

merge: blog-api development branch into production

Complete production-ready personal blog REST API in Go.

Features implemented:
- JWT auth (login, register, refresh, logout, forgot/reset password)

- User management with RBAC (superadmin, admin, editor, viewer)

- Audit logging for all admin activity

- DB migrations via golang-migrate (runs on startup)

Security:
- Password complexity (ISO-aligned: 12+ chars, mixed case, digits, symbols)
- hCaptcha support
- XSS sanitization on all inputs
- Input trimming and validation
- Superadmin escalation guard
- bcrypt password hashing with dedicated UpdatePasswordHash method

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix UNICODE_CASE flag alone enabling case folding (#124)

UNICODE_CASE without CASE_INSENSITIVE was incorrectly enabling
case-insensitive matching because toParseFlags() mapped it to
ParseFlags.FOLD_CASE | ParseFlags.UNICODE_GROUPS. Now it only maps to
ParseFlags.UNICODE_GROUPS, matching JDK behavior where UNICODE_CASE
alone has no effect on matching.

Fixes #114

commit.message:
feat(dreamion): full implementation — dream state engine (34 lines → 595 lines)

From stub (34 lines) to complete engine (595 lines C + 204 lines header):

Architecture:
  LIGHT mode (idle > 500 ticks):
    - TASK_DEDUP: scan token-pair ring buffer, flag duplicate prompt prefixes
    - TASK_COMPACT: gist token frequency analysis from memory ring
    - TASK_PREDICT: pre-warm 8 probable next prompts from recent sessions

  DEEP mode (idle > 2000 ticks):
    - TASK_SYNTH: synthetic self-play pairs (LCG blending of 2 memories)
    - TASK_DNA_MUTATE: compute bias_delta/temp_delta from domain distribution
    - TASK_CONSOLIDATE: long-term memory merge + FNV dream_hash
    - TASK_FLUSH_JSONL: batch write training data to OO_DREAM.BIN pipeline

Key subsystems:
  DreamionMemory ring (64 slots) — records prompt/output/confidence/domain
  DreamionPrediction ring (8 slots) — precomputed next-prompt candidates
  DreamionStats — full telemetry (synth_pairs, dna_mutations, wakes...)
  DreamionJsonlBuf (32 lines) — batch JSONL with flush callback
  Auto-transitions: OFF→LIGHT at 500 ticks, LIGHT→DEEP at 2000 ticks
  Auto-wake: dreamion_tick_active() wakes on DREAMION_WAKE_THRESH input ticks
  dreamion_pop_dna_mutation() → caller applies delta to SomaDNA

Integration (soma_repl.c):
  - dreamion_tick() called every REPL iteration (idle count)
  - dreamion_tick_active() called on user input (wake detection)
  - dreamion_step() runs one task per idle iteration
  - DNA mutation applied in inference layer

soma_boot.c: status printer updated (cycles → stats.total_dream_cycles)

No libc, no malloc, no floating-point printf — fully freestanding C11.

commit.message:
fix: prevent cold-start SN0 parking on container restart

Two-pronged fix for the bug where restarting Docker containers caused
data-dependent strategies to swap everything into SN0 (root network):

1. Scheduler hydration: call preparePreview() before the initial
   startup rebalance so strategies have indicator histories loaded
   from their persistent SQLite databases.

2. Cold-start skip signal: add skipReason to StrategyResult so
   strategies can abort a cycle when zero winners resulted from
   insufficient data (not genuine market conditions).

   sma-stoploss: detects when ALL gate-passing subnets lack SMA
   price history (cold start) vs some being bearish (legitimate).
   Also fixes leaked DB connection in preparePreview().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: write-behind sled buffer in masternode registry to prevent tokio stall

Replace direct synchronous sled writes inside masternodes.write().await
with a fire-and-forget channel to a background writer task. Under gossip
flood, multiple tasks compete for the async write lock; each previously
held it while doing sled I/O (insert/remove), starving tokio workers and
causing RPC timeouts → watchdog restart.

All sled writes inside register_internal, unregister, cleanup_inactive,
record_reward, and increment_blocks_without_reward now send SledWriteOp
to an unbounded channel drained by a background tokio task. Sled reads
inside the lock are unchanged (fast, non-blocking).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: add compute shader system value lowering blueprint

Blueprint for mapping Vulkan compute system values to Evergreen hardware:
- gl_WorkGroupID -> TGID (SPI VGPR input, TGID_ENA)
- gl_LocalInvocationID -> TID (SPI VGPR input, TID_IN_GROUP_ENA)
- gl_WorkGroupSize -> compile-time constant (immediate literal)
- gl_NumWorkGroups -> KCACHE bank 15 driver prefix (bytes 16-27)
- gl_GlobalInvocationID -> ALU synthesis (TGID * WorkGroupSize + TID)

Includes SPI_COMPUTE_INPUT_CNTL register programming, VGPR layout,
RAT/CB compute integration (CB0-8 full + CB9-11 RAT-only), and
explicit documentation that TGID/TID must NOT be collapsed to CB reads.

commit.message:
feat: snap constraints, version-aware components, reusable SnapCard (#95)

* remove button_index from snap POST payload

Buttons are now distinguished by their submit target URLs (e.g.
`?action=save` vs `?action=cancel`) instead of a positional index in
the signed payload.

`payloadSchema` uses `.strip()` so old clients that still send
`button_index` won't break — the field is silently dropped during
validation rather than rejected.

Made-with: Cursor

* add nonce + audience to snap POST payload and validate origin

Adds anti-replay `nonce` and `audience` fields to the JFS payload
schema. `parseRequest` now checks that the payload `audience` matches
the request origin (derived from the request URL or provided via
`requestOrigin` option), rejecting mismatches with a new
`origin_mismatch` error type. Emulators generate nonce/audience when
building POST payloads. `snapOriginFromRequest` in snap-hono falls
back to the request URL instead of a hardcoded docs URL.

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix stale input normalization docs to match current parseRequest behavior

Made-with: Cursor
